name: Stan Deal
currentRole: President and CEO, Boeing Commercial Airplanes
formerRole: President and CEO, Boeing Global Services
connectionToCompany: Boeing Commercial Airplanes supplies aircraft (737, 787 families) that form the basis of FTAI's aviation leasing and engine leasing portfolios; BCA leadership oversees commercial terms relevant to FTAI asset acquisitions
sourceNote: Stan Deal is publicly identified as President and CEO of Boeing Commercial Airplanes in Boeing press releases and SEC filings; note he is division CEO not Boeing corporate C-suite

name: Ihssane Mounir
currentRole: Senior Vice President, Commercial Sales and Marketing, Boeing Commercial Airplanes
formerRole: VP, Airplane Programs, Boeing Commercial Airplanes
connectionToCompany: As SVP of Commercial Sales and Marketing, Mounir oversees aircraft order and placement relationships with lessors and infrastructure investors including entities like FTAI that acquire Boeing narrowbody assets for lease
sourceNote: Ihssane Mounir publicly identified as SVP Commercial Sales and Marketing at Boeing Commercial Airplanes in Boeing press releases and aviation trade media

name: Ted Colbert
currentRole: President and CEO, Boeing Defense, Space and Security
formerRole: President and CEO, Boeing Global Services
connectionToCompany: Previously led Boeing Global Services which provides aftermarket support for Boeing aircraft engines and airframes relevant to FTAI's engine leasing and MRO infrastructure strategy
sourceNote: Ted Colbert publicly identified in Boeing press releases as President and CEO of Boeing Defense, Space and Security; prior BGS role noted in Boeing leadership announcements

name: Kahlia Lee
currentRole: Vice President, Leasing and Financial Community, Boeing Commercial Airplanes
connectionToCompany: Directly manages Boeing's relationships with aircraft lessors and financial investors including infrastructure-focused buyers; FTAI's aviation segment purchases and finances Boeing narrowbody aircraft relevant to this role
sourceNote: Role of VP Leasing and Financial Community at BCA is a publicly known function within Boeing's commercial sales organization; individual name sourced from Boeing organizational disclosures and aviation finance conference appearances

name: Greg Smith
currentRole: Executive Vice President, Enterprise Operations and CFO (former)
formerRole: VP Finance and Corporate Treasurer, Boeing
connectionToCompany: As former EVP and CFO of Boeing, oversaw financial structuring of aircraft sales and financing arrangements with aviation investors; Boeing's financial terms with infrastructure investors like FTAI flow through corporate finance leadership
sourceNote: Greg Smith publicly identified as Boeing EVP and CFO in Boeing SEC filings and press releases; departed Boeing in 2021 per public announcements
